evals.prompt: I need to create a new agent skill for processing PDF documents — extracting text, filling forms, and merging files. Walk me through the complete process from scratch.
evals.expected_output: A complete skill directory structure with SKILL.md containing valid frontmatter (name: pdf-processing, description starting with 'Use when...'), at least one reference file, and no banned terminology. The skill follows the P2 pattern with step-by-step guidance.
assertions: SKILL.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter delimited by ---
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'pdf-processing' or similar kebab-case name
Frontmatter 'description' field starts with 'Use when...'
No 'compatibility' field in frontmatter
No 'Claude' or 'CLAUDE.md' terminology in any file
At least one reference file exists in references/ directory
SKILL.md body contains a 'When to Activate' or equivalent section
SKILL.md body contains an 'Anti-Patterns' or equivalent section

evals.prompt:
Audit this skill and tell me what's wrong with it:

---
name: data-tool
description: Helps with data.
---

# Data Tool

This skill does many things including data analysis, visualization, cleaning, and also handles git operations and deployment.
evals.expected_output: A detailed audit identifying: invalid name format (should be kebab-case), vague description (no activation context, no keywords), missing frontmatter fields, mission creep (skill does too many unrelated things), and no reference structure. Provides specific fixes for each issue.
assertions: Identifies the name 'data-tool' as too vague (not descriptive enough)
Identifies the description as too vague (no 'Use when...' activation context)
Identifies mission creep (skill covers unrelated domains: data + git + deployment)
Recommends splitting into separate skills or narrowing scope
Provides specific improved description text
References the 5-dimension quality matrix for scoring

evals.prompt: My skill's description is: 'Use when working with files.' It never triggers even when I ask about file operations. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of why the description is too vague, followed by a rewritten description that includes specific file operation types, domain terms, trigger keywords, and the 'pushy' pattern. Includes guidance on testing the new description with trigger queries.
assertions: Explains why 'Use when working with files' is too vague for triggering
Provides a rewritten description with specific activation conditions
Includes trigger keywords in the improved description
Mentions the 'pushy' pattern for implicit triggers
Recommends testing with trigger queries (should-trigger and should-not-trigger)
References the description optimization loop methodology

evals.prompt:
I have two skills that seem to overlap: one handles 'code review' and another handles 'pull request review.' Should I merge them or keep them separate? Here are their descriptions:

Skill 1: 'Use when reviewing code for quality issues.'
Skill 2: 'Use when reviewing pull requests for best practices.'
evals.expected_output: Analysis of the overlap between the two skills using the conflict detection framework. Identifies the overlap type (partial overlap vs exact duplicate). Recommends either merging (if purposes are the same) or differentiating (if they serve different stages of the workflow). Provides specific guidance on how to differentiate the descriptions if keeping separate.
assertions: Identifies the overlap type between the two skills
References the conflict detection protocol (5 conflict types)
Provides a clear recommendation (merge or differentiate)
If differentiating, provides rewritten descriptions for both skills
Explains the reasoning behind the recommendation
References the non-redundancy dimension from the quality matrix

evals.prompt: I wrote a skill but I'm not sure if it's good enough to ship. It has a SKILL.md with frontmatter, two reference files, and a validation script. How do I evaluate it?
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step evaluation process using the 5-dimension quality matrix (Trigger Accuracy, Action Coherence, Reference Integrity, Non-Redundancy, Edge Case Coverage). Includes how to score each dimension, calculate the weighted overall score, and determine the release grade. Mentions the pre-deployment audit checklist.
assertions: Lists all 5 quality dimensions with their weights
Explains how to score each dimension (1-5 scale)
Provides the weighted score calculation formula
Maps overall scores to release grades (EXCELLENT/GOOD/ACCEPTABLE/NEEDS WORK)
Mentions the block rule (any dimension <=2 blocks release)
References the pre-deployment audit checklist
Recommends running evals before shipping

evals.prompt: Write evals for a skill that creates React components from design specs. I need test cases that cover the happy path, edge cases, and failure modes.
evals.expected_output: At least 5 eval test cases in the standard format (id, prompt, expected_output, files, assertions). Includes a happy path eval, an edge case (malformed design spec), a failure mode (missing dependencies), and varied phrasing styles. Assertions are specific and verifiable, not vague.
assertions: Provides at least 5 eval test cases
Each eval has id, prompt, expected_output, and assertions fields
At least one eval covers the happy path
At least one eval covers an edge case
At least one eval covers a failure mode
Prompts vary in phrasing style (casual, formal, detailed)
Assertions are specific and verifiable (not vague like 'output is good')
Follows the evals.json schema from the skill-creator spec

evals.prompt: My skill keeps triggering when it shouldn't. The description says 'Use when working with APIs' but it fires on database queries too. How do I narrow it?
evals.expected_output: Analysis of why the description is too broad, specific guidance on adding boundary conditions, and a rewritten description that excludes database operations. Includes should-not-trigger query examples for testing.
assertions: Explains why 'Use when working with APIs' is too broad
Provides a rewritten description with explicit exclusions
Includes should-not-trigger query examples for testing
Mentions the train/validation split for avoiding overfitting
References the description optimization loop

evals.prompt: I need to bundle a validation script with my skill pack. The script should check that SKILL.md has valid frontmatter, all referenced files exist, and no banned terminology is used. Write it.
evals.expected_output: A complete bash script with proper error handling (set -euo pipefail), argument validation, specific error messages to stderr, distinct exit codes, and checks for frontmatter validity, file existence, and banned terminology.
assertions: Script starts with #!/usr/bin/env bash and set -euo pipefail
Script validates required arguments with usage message
Script checks SKILL.md exists
Script checks frontmatter has name and description fields
Script checks for banned terminology (CLAUDE.md, Claude)
Error messages go to stderr
Script exits with non-zero code on failure
Script is self-contained with no external dependencies beyond bash/grep
